findings: Backward packet length and segment size features exhibit substantial class-conditioned structural variation, indicating variable features contribute to class discrimination.
Backward bulk and flag features show near-constant values with extremely low cardinality, indicating stable features contribute minimal class discrimination.
Tension exists locally between stable low-diversity features and variable class-conditioned features, complicating interpretation of feature stability and redundancy.
contradictions: Presence of duplicated rows conflicts with observed class-conditioned structural variation and strong feature dependencies, complicating interpretation of feature stability and redundancy.
Coexistence of near-constant features with strongly variable features creates unresolved tension between stable and variable feature subsets.
limitations: No dependency contextualization or relation verification was performed on the low-diversity backward bulk and flag features due to bounded scope and budget.
Local evidence acquisition did not resolve the tension between stable and variable feature subsets fully.
